question: Will CPI rise more than 1.1% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, showing that CPI increased only 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis. This is significantly below the 1.1% threshold required for a YES resolution. Since the official data is already available and shows 0.3% < 1.1%, the market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty.
key_factors: Official BLS data already released showing 0.3% monthly increase
No uncertainty remaining since official government data is available
Market closes tomorrow but result is already determined
risks: Extremely minimal risk of data revision
Possible misunderstanding of market resolution criteria
Technical issues with market resolution process
